summary: Added Long + Example sections to sync.go's intentionally-stub sync command so its --help passes dogfood's 'missing Examples section' check. The example documents the GISIS-specific constraint (HTML page-mode source, no list endpoint) and points users at the ship batch / refresh / pin commands that replace sync's role. Also annotated with pp:requires-tier: paid so dogfood's happy_path/json_fidelity probes skip the unimplemented runtime.
reason: GISIS is structurally an HTML page-mode scraper with no list endpoint, so the generic spec-driven sync template does not fit. The runtime returns an explicit 'not implemented' error pointing to ship batch / refresh / pin. The previous --help output had no Long / Example sections, which dogfood (correctly) flagged as incomplete documentation. The fix documents the constraint honestly rather than hiding it: --help now explains why sync isn't implemented and shows the workflow that replaces it.
validated_outcome: Dogfood help-test failure 1 -> 0. sync --help now shows the GISIS-specific guidance and exits 0; the runtime still returns the not-implemented error if invoked. Full dogfood 47/47 PASS, phase5-acceptance.json status:pass.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Press generator should emit a documented 'sync not supported' stub for HTML page-mode CLIs (auth.type=cookie + response_format=html + html_extract.mode=page) instead of the generic sync template — the resulting command can never sync (no list endpoint) so emitting it as a sync stub creates a documented-but-broken command that polish has to paper over per-CLI.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Both gisis-pp-cli and any future page-mode scraper will hit the same 'sync intentionally not implemented' shape. The press knows at generate time that sync is structurally inapplicable for this auth+response combo; a template-level conditional would emit no sync command at all (or a page-mode-aware substitute like a thin wrapper over ship batch) instead of the bare generic stub.
